---
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Find project root and make src/ importable
# ------------------------------------------------------------
PROJECT_DIR = Path.cwd()

while not (PROJECT_DIR / "src").exists() and PROJECT_DIR != PROJECT_DIR.parent:
    PROJECT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR.parent

if not (PROJECT_DIR / "src").exists():
    raise FileNotFoundError("Could not find project root containing src/ folder.")

sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_DIR))

import yaml
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display, HTML

from src.cb3_utils import load_cb3_tract_universe
from src.map_utils import add_metric_layer, make_base_map

DOMAIN = "education"

SCHOOL_LEVEL_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "EDUCATION_DOMAIN_school_level_analytic_file.csv"
TRACT_LEVEL_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "EDUCATION_DOMAIN_tract_level_analytic_file.csv"
CLEAN_OUTPUT_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "education.csv"
METADATA_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "docs" / "metric_metadata.yml"
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "maps" / "education"
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
```


## Page-level CSS

```{python}
# Hide Quarto title chrome and shrink Leaflet controls, matching the other
# domain map notebooks.
display(
    HTML(
        """
        <style>
        h1.title { display: none; }
        .quarto-title-block { display: none; }
        .cell-output-stdout { display: none; }
        .leaflet-control-container .legend,
        .leaflet-control-container .branca,
        .leaflet-control-container .colormap,
        .leaflet-control-container .leaflet-control {
            font-size: 12px;
        }
        .education-map-title {
            font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
            font-size: 18px;
            font-weight: 700;
            margin-top: 24px;
            margin-bottom: 8px;
        }
        </style>
        """
    )
)
```


## Load the official 31-tract CB3 universe and geometry

```{python}
tracts_lookup, cb3_tract_geometry, CB3_TRACT_CODES, CB3_GEOIDS = load_cb3_tract_universe(PROJECT_DIR)
```


## Load tract-level under-5 population

School level data does not have `GEOID`, so have to construct to generate the key.

```{python}
def load_tract_population(path):
    """Load the tract-level ACS extract and return GEOID plus under-5 population.

    Builds GEOID from the state/county/tract columns (same convention as
    src.cb3_utils.add_acs_geoid) and validates against the 31-tract CB3 universe.
    """
    frame = pd.read_csv(path)
    frame["GEOID"] = (
        frame["state"].astype(int).astype(str).str.zfill(2)
        + frame["county"].astype(int).astype(str).str.zfill(3)
        + frame["tract"].astype(int).astype(str).str.zfill(6)
    )
    assert set(frame["GEOID"]) == CB3_GEOIDS, "Tract-level file does not match the 31 CB3 tracts."
    return frame[["GEOID", "tot_under_5"]]


tract_population = load_tract_population(TRACT_LEVEL_PATH)
```

**Summary:** Reads the tract-level ACS extract, rebuilds the standard 11-digit
GEOID from `state`/`county`/`tract`, and keeps only the under-5 population
column needed for the seats-vs-population ratio. Verify: the assertion
confirms all 31 CB3 tracts are present with no extras.


## Load school-level metrics and flag any schools outside the 31 CB3 tracts

```{python}
def load_school_metrics(path, cb3_geoids):
    """Load the school-level file and assign each school a tract GEOID.

    All CB3 schools are in New York County (state 36, county 061), so GEOID is
    built directly from the school-level `tract` column (zero-padded to 6
    digits) rather than from state/county columns, which are not present in
    this file. Schools whose tract code does not match one of the 31 CB3
    tracts are flagged and excluded from the tract aggregation, since we do
    not have coordinates to geocode them as a fallback.
    """
    frame = pd.read_csv(path)
    frame["GEOID"] = "36061" + frame["tract"].astype(int).astype(str).str.zfill(6)

    unmatched = frame.loc[~frame["GEOID"].isin(cb3_geoids)]
    if len(unmatched):
        display(
            HTML(
                "<p><strong>Flagged:</strong> "
                f"{len(unmatched)} school(s) have a tract code that does not match "
                "any of the 31 CB3 tracts and are excluded from the tract "
                "aggregation below (see 'name', 'tract' columns):</p>"
                + unmatched[["name", "tract", "GEOID"]].to_html(index=False)
            )
        )

    return frame.loc[frame["GEOID"].isin(cb3_geoids)].copy()


school_metrics = load_school_metrics(SCHOOL_LEVEL_PATH, CB3_GEOIDS)
```

**Summary:** Assigns each school a GEOID from its `tract` column and drops
schools that don't match a CB3 tract, displaying them for review. Verify:
check the flagged table above — currently one school (tract code `4000`,
which does not match the split tracts `4001`/`4002`) is expected to appear
and is excluded rather than guessed at.


## Aggregate school-level metrics to the tract level

```{python}
def aggregate_school_metrics_to_tract(school_frame, cb3_geoids):
    """Sum school-level metrics up to one row per CB3 tract.

    Uses sum(min_count=1) rather than plain sum, since pandas' default sum
    treats an all-missing group as summing to 0 rather than NaN — which would
    otherwise turn "no school in this tract reported this metric" into a
    false "$0 raised" or "0 students in temporary housing." A school with no
    PreK program contributes a real 0 to the tract total (it's not missing
    data), but a school that simply didn't report PTA revenue contributes
    nothing countable. Tracts with no schools at all also get NaN for every
    metric, for the same reason. PreK and 3-K seats are kept as separate
    metrics rather than combined, since they are different programs.
    """
    grouped = school_frame.groupby("GEOID").agg(
        school_count=("GEOID", "size"),
        pta_fundraising_total=("pta_tot_inc", lambda values: values.sum(min_count=1)),
        n_students_temp_housing_total=("n_students_temp_housing", lambda values: values.sum(min_count=1)),
        prek_seats_total=("prek_seats_2122", lambda values: values.sum(min_count=1)),
        threek_seats_total=("threek_seats_2122", lambda values: values.sum(min_count=1)),
    )

    # Reindex to all 31 CB3 tracts so tracts with zero schools are represented.
    grouped = grouped.reindex(sorted(cb3_geoids))
    grouped["school_count"] = grouped["school_count"].fillna(0)

    no_school_mask = grouped["school_count"] == 0
    metric_columns = [
        "pta_fundraising_total",
        "n_students_temp_housing_total",
        "prek_seats_total",
        "threek_seats_total",
    ]
    grouped.loc[no_school_mask, metric_columns] = pd.NA

    return grouped.reset_index()


tract_school_metrics = aggregate_school_metrics_to_tract(school_metrics, CB3_GEOIDS)
```

**Summary:** Groups the 41 matched schools by tract, summing PTA income,
students in temporary housing, PreK seats, and 3-K seats (kept separate),
then reindexes to all 31 CB3 tracts so tracts with no school show up as
missing (gray on the map) rather than zero. Verify: spot-check
`school_count` against a known multi-school tract, and confirm tracts with
`school_count == 0` show `NaN` for all four metric columns.


## Combine into one tract-level education table and compute the seats ratio

```{python}
def build_education_tract_table(tract_school_metrics, tract_population):
    """Join aggregated school metrics to under-5 population and compute the
    PreK and 3-K seats coverage ratios (seats per 100 children under 5), kept
    as two separate metrics since PreK and 3-K are different programs.
    """
    combined = tract_school_metrics.merge(tract_population, on="GEOID", how="left", validate="one_to_one")
    combined["prek_seats_per_100_under5"] = combined["prek_seats_total"] / combined["tot_under_5"] * 100
    combined["threek_seats_per_100_under5"] = combined["threek_seats_total"] / combined["tot_under_5"] * 100
    return combined


education_tracts = build_education_tract_table(tract_school_metrics, tract_population)
education_tracts.to_csv(CLEAN_OUTPUT_PATH, index=False)
```

**Summary:** Merges the tract-level school aggregates with under-5
population and computes PreK and 3-K seats per 100 under-5 children as two
separate columns, then writes the one-row-per-tract table to
`data/clean/education.csv`. Verify: confirm the output has exactly 31 rows
and that both ratio columns are `NaN` wherever the corresponding seats total
is `NaN` (no schools in tract).


## Join metrics to tract geometry for mapping

```{python}
tracts = cb3_tract_geometry.merge(education_tracts, on="GEOID", how="left", validate="one_to_one")
tracts = tracts.merge(
    tracts_lookup[["GEOID", "tract_label", "nta_name"]],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="one_to_one",
)

assert len(tracts) == 31, f"Expected 31 CB3 tracts, got {len(tracts)}"
assert tracts["GEOID"].is_unique, "GEOID is not unique after merge."
```


## Load metric metadata and select education metrics to map

```{python}
with open(METADATA_PATH) as f:
    all_metrics = yaml.safe_load(f)

metric_specs = {
    var: meta
    for var, meta in all_metrics.items()
    if meta.get("domain") == DOMAIN
}

metrics_to_map = [
    "pta_fundraising_total",
    "n_students_temp_housing_total",
    "prek_seats_per_100_under5",
    "threek_seats_per_100_under5",
]

metrics_to_map = [
    metric
    for metric in metrics_to_map
    if metric in metric_specs and metric in tracts.columns
]

if not metrics_to_map:
    raise ValueError(
        "No education metrics found to map. Check docs/metric_metadata.yml "
        "and data/clean/education.csv."
    )
```


## Create one map per metric

```{python}
for metric in metrics_to_map:
    meta = metric_specs[metric]

    display(
        HTML(
            f"""
            <div class="education-map-title">
                {meta["label"]}
            </div>
            """
        )
    )

    spec = {
        "dimension": meta["short_label"],
        "label": meta["label"],
        "unit": meta["unit"],
        "palette": meta["palette"],
    }

    for optional_key in ["positive_values_only", "zero_color", "zero_label"]:
        if optional_key in meta:
            spec[optional_key] = meta[optional_key]

    m = make_base_map(tracts)

    add_metric_layer(
        m,
        tracts,
        metric,
        spec,
        show=True,
        overlay=False,
    )

    m.save(MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / meta["filename"])
    display(m)
```
